title: Human Raindrop Velocimeter
definition: The inherent ability of humans to subconsciously estimate cloud speeds based on the angle and pattern of falling raindrops.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Guessing the speed of clouds based on raindrop patterns is something humans can do without thinking.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The human brain has a unique ability to estimate cloud speeds by observing the angle and pattern of falling raindrops.
difficulty_levels.expert: Humans have an undiscovered passive observation network that can detect changes in cloud speed through a natural calibration process.

extra_fields.insight: Humans have an undiscovered passive observation network that can detect changes in cloud speed through a natural calibration process.
extra_fields.historical_context: The Human Raindrop Velocimeter is an interesting development in the field of psychology and perception studies.
limitations: Understanding the exact mechanism behind this phenomenon remains a mystery.
It's unclear how accurate these subconscious estimates are.
applications: Weather forecasting (indirectly) by crowdsourcing cloud speed estimates.
Research in human perception and cognition.
